goal: List the files in the current directory and summarize the project structure
summary: Gorechera is a Go-based stateful multi-agent orchestration engine. The project follows standard Go layout: cmd/ for CLI entry point, internal/ for core packages (api, domain, orchestrator, policy, provider, runtime, schema, store), docs/ for architecture and design documents, and examples/ for sample configs. The codebase contains ~30 Go source files across 8 internal packages, with a prebuilt gorechera.exe binary at root. This is a read-only reconnaissance task requiring directory listing and file enumeration only.
product_scope: Enumerate all files and directories in the workspace root
Recursively list the project tree structure
Summarize the purpose of each top-level directory and key files
Identify the package layout and architectural organization
non_goals: Do not build or test the project
Do not analyze code logic or find bugs
proposed_steps: Step 1: List root directory contents (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, Old/, cmd/, docs/, examples/, go.mod, gorechera.exe, internal/)
Step 2: Recursively list all files under cmd/, internal/, docs/, examples/ to build a complete file tree
Step 3: Read key files (go.mod, README.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) to understand module name, dependencies, and architectural overview

Step 4: Produce a structured summary: root files, cmd/gorechera/main.go (CLI entry), internal/ packages (api, domain, orchestrator, policy, provider, runtime, schema, store), docs/ (6 markdown files), examples/ (sample config)
success_signals: Package structure and entry points documented

verification_contract.goal: Verify that the file listing is complete and the project summary is accurate
required_checks: All top-level entries present: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, Old/, cmd/, docs/, examples/, go.mod, gorechera.exe, internal/
Summary mentions all 8 internal packages: api, domain, orchestrator, policy, provider, runtime, schema, store
No files were modified (git status clean or no changes)
verification_contract.notes: Pure read-only task. Verification should confirm listing completeness by cross-checking with independent directory enumeration.
